target: examples/binder.yaml (protein-anything, de-novo binder vs chain A of 7ROA)
metric: designfolding-bb_rmsd (scRMSD, isolated refold, Kabsch CA-RMSD in Angstrom)

reference_run.weights: HuggingFace boltzgen/boltzgen-1, snapshot c1be29e1f82ffcc72264f64b993c43fb4e0d17f0, boltzgen1_diverse.ckpt (design) + boltz2_conf_final.ckpt (design_folding refold). Public, unauthenticated pull.
reference_run.settings: protocol=protein-anything, recycling_steps=3, sampling_steps=500, diffusion_samples=1, bf16-mixed (pytorch-lightning AMP), use_kernels=auto, devices=1, --no_subprocess; matches the device design.yaml defaults exactly. BoltzGen exposes no --seed flag, so each invocation is an independent draw (same discipline as the device leg).

device_vs_reference_parity.verdict: device designability meets-or-exceeds the reference (device <=2A pass-rate 93.75% vs reference 68.75%; device median scRMSD 0.78 A vs reference 1.05 A). The device sits on the favorable side of the reference's own batch-to-batch spread (reference batch_a 75% vs batch_b 62.5% <=2A, a 12.5pp band at n=8). The port does not regress designability. No 1:1 output correspondence (BoltzGen designs new sequences), so parity is in the designability distribution, not a per-design RMSD.
device_vs_reference_parity.caveat: n=16 per side is small; the reference's own two batches are 12.5pp apart on the <=2A bar, so part of the device-reference gap is sampling noise. The direction (device >= reference) is consistent across both batch pairs.

reference_blocker_history.note: Prior state (2026-07-13, before the rented-GPU leg): the reference leg was blocked on pc, which has no NVIDIA GPU. Resolved by renting an on-demand RTX 3090 on vast.ai and running the official CLI there. Kept for provenance.
reference_blocker_history.verified_on_host: pc (no NVIDIA GPU: AMD Phoenix APU only, lspci-confirmed)
reference_blocker_history.crash: AssertionError: Torch not compiled with CUDA enabled
reference_blocker_history.crash_site: src/boltzgen/cli/boltzgen.py:921, PipelineBuilder.__init__: device_capability = torch.cuda.get_device_capability() -- called unconditionally before any CLI arg (--use_kernels, --devices) is consulted
reference_blocker_history.reproduction: pip install torch (CPU wheel) in a scratch venv; torch.cuda.get_device_capability() raises the exact assertion above, confirming no CLI flag or config override can route around it without patching upstream source
